name: Easter Lamb Soup

ingredients.item: About 6 cups chicken stock, as needed
cup each chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley and dill
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper

Put the lamb shank in a large saucepan with 3 quarts cold water. Bring to a simmer over high heat, skimming any foam. Cover partially and adjust the heat to maintain a gentle simmer. Cook until the lamb is fork-tender, about 2 hours. Lift the shank from the broth to a plate and let cool; reserve the broth. Pull the meat from the lamb shank and chop coarsely.

Trim any fat from the tripe and scrub the tripe with the lemon halves. Rinse well. Put the tripe and lamb hearts in a large pot with the celery and add water to cover generously. Bring to a simmer, skimming any foam. Adjust the heat to maintain a gentle simmer and cook for 30 minutes, then drain. When cool enough to handle, cut the tripe and lamb hearts into bite-sized pieces.

Add enough chicken stock to the reserved lamb broth to measure 3 quarts. Add the tripe, lamb shank meat, chopped lamb hearts, scallions, parsley, dill, 1 tablespoon salt, and several grinds of pepper. Simmer, partially covered, until the tripe is tender, about 1
hours. Add the rice and simmer, partially covered, until tender, about 20 minutes.

In a bowl, whisk the eggs until pale and frothy. Gradually whisk in the lemon juice, then gradually whisk in several cups of the hot broth from the pot. Return this mixture to the pot and stir it in gently so as not to disturb the foam. Do not allow the soup to boil after adding the egg mixture. Taste and adjust the seasoning, then serve immediately in warm bowls.

method: Put the lamb shank in a large saucepan with 3 quarts cold water. Bring to a simmer over high heat, skimming any foam. Cover partially and adjust the heat to maintain a gentle simmer. Cook until the lamb is fork-tender, about 2 hours. Lift the shank from the broth to a plate and let cool; reserve the broth. Pull the meat from the lamb shank and chop coarsely.

Trim any fat from the tripe and scrub the tripe with the lemon halves. Rinse well. Put the tripe and lamb hearts in a large pot with the celery and add water to cover generously. Bring to a simmer, skimming any foam. Adjust the heat to maintain a gentle simmer and cook for 30 minutes, then drain. When cool enough to handle, cut the tripe and lamb hearts into bite-sized pieces.

Add enough chicken stock to the reserved lamb broth to measure 3 quarts. Add the tripe, lamb shank meat, chopped lamb hearts, scallions, parsley, dill, 1 tablespoon salt, and several grinds of pepper. Simmer, partially covered, until the tripe is tender, about 11/2 hours. Add the rice and simmer, partially covered, until tender, about 20 minutes.

In a bowl, whisk the eggs until pale and frothy. Gradually whisk in the lemon juice, then gradually whisk in several cups of the hot broth from the pot. Return this mixture to the pot and stir it in gently so as not to disturb the foam. Do not allow the soup to boil after adding the egg mixture. Taste and adjust the seasoning, then serve immediately in warm bowls.
